title: Procurement
description: The page explains the importance of using secure and official U.S. government websites, emphasizing the significance of .gov domains and HTTPS connections for safely sharing sensitive information.
text:
LLNL Procurement
At LLNL, Procurement plays a vital role in procuring the goods and services essential for advancing our cutting-edge science and technology. We are committed to purchasing high-quality supplies and services in a cost-effective and timely manner, ensuring effective competition while adhering to socioeconomic compliance and applicable DOE regulations outlined in our Prime Contract with the US Department of Energy (DOE) National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA).
About LLNL
Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory (LLNL) is a national laboratory for science and technology solutions to the most pressing national and global security challenges. LLNL is managed by Lawrence Livermore National Security (LLNS) which is a limited liability company (LLC) that assumed management of the Lab on October 1, 2007. The LLNS management team includes Bechtel National, Regents of the University of California, Babcock and Wilcox Company, URS Corporation, and Battelle. LLNL is a government-owned contractor-operated facility managed through a contract between LLNS and the Department of Energy’s National Nuclear Security Administration.
Visit the LLNL website and familiarize yourself with LLNL's strategic missions, world-class programs, and capabilities.
